arcs.title: The Third Fold
arcs.summary: Steve has briefed Christine on the full story: a 1962 four-foot elevation dispute between two survey teams, both irreconcilable, both unreturned. He rates his own theory at seven out of ten. Christine has the survey notes — he printed them in March. Neither party has yet said aloud that Orvell's brass duck coordinates and the Third Fold appear to point to the same ridgeline, but both parties are aware of it. Day 3 should cover that acknowledgment and the first real planning — or an unexpected complication at the disputed ridgeline that the brass duck's coordinates hint at.
